steps.title: Make two points for the line
steps.content: The point $(0,12)$ is already on the line. The slope is $2$, so when $x$ increases by $1$, $y$ increases by $2$. That gives a second point, $(1,14)$.

steps.title: Use the points directly
steps.content: Use the table points directly. From $(0,12)$ to $(1,14)$, the slope is $m=\frac{14-12}{1-0}=2.$ Substituting a point into $y=mx+b$ gives $b=12$. This matches choice D — D is correct.
